episodes.title: The Korean Levered ETFs Shaking Markets All Around the World
episodes.summary:
**Summary**
Odd Lots speaks with Barclays' Alex Altman ("Altie") about the explosive growth of single-stock levered ETFs, using this week's Korea-driven market action (Samsung earnings, SK Hynix's US listing) as the entry point. The conversation matters for an EM trader because Korea has become "ground zero" for retail-driven leverage that is now materially amplifying underlying share moves and creating mechanical, non-discretionary flow in global markets.

**Key points**
- Global levered-ETF AUM peaked near $250-270bn; APAC AUM tripled from $12-13bn to $50-55bn, while US AUM rose from ~$120bn (April lows) to north of $200bn.
- Korean AUM growth has come mostly from new share creation/inflows (retail buying), unlike the US where growth is mostly price-driven; 93% of Korean levered ETFs are retail-owned vs ~75% in the US.
- Mechanics: triple-levered funds must rebalance daily (sell more on down days, buy more on up days), creating a growing short-gamma dimension in equity markets that partially offsets long-gamma overwriting flows.
- Leverage is sourced via swaps with prime brokers, contributing to (but not the main driver of) tighter bank balance sheets/higher financing rates — multi-manager hedge funds (~$1trn AUM, tripled since Covid) are cited as the bigger balance-sheet user.
- US household equity exposure is 34% of wealth (record high, above dot-com), vs 26% in real estate — the widest gap on record, raising the stakes of any sharp equity drawdown for US consumption.
- Barclays' quant "Betty" model (19 inputs, no sentiment) has flagged record overbought/momentum-crowded conditions since late May, implying weak two-month forward S&P asymmetry, though earnings and fiscal deficit spending are cushioning downside.
- 10yr real yields near 95th percentile (~2.3%) argue for S&P multiples of 14-18x vs current ~20x — a valuation gap attributed partly to record-high margins.

**FX / rates implications**
- No direct EM FX/rates calls made, but Korea's retail-leveraged equity dynamics are a channel to watch for KRW volatility and capital-flow swings tied to chip-sector single stocks (Samsung, SK Hynix).
- Elevated US real yields and record IG bond issuance signal ongoing competition for capital that could pressure risk assets broadly, an indirect headwind for EM carry trades if it triggers a US equity correction.
- A disorderly US equity drawdown (16trn/50% GDP-equivalent wealth impairment scenario discussed) would be a global risk-off trigger with spillover to EM FX.

**Watch list**
- SK Hynix's US listing and ongoing Korean single-stock levered ETF flows for signs of feedback loops into underlying shares.
- Barclays' Betty indicator for shifts out of overbought/momentum-crowded territory.
- Bank balance-sheet capacity/financing rates as a stress point if leverage and multi-strat AUM keep growing.
